frameworks.name: two-photon calcium imaging
frameworks.one_line: longitudinal optical readout of single-neuron activity during behavior

frameworks.name: voluntary head-fixation
frameworks.one_line: magnetic failsafe restraint that animals self-engage

core_question: Can voluntary magnetic head-fixation enable stable lifespan two-photon imaging of hippocampal neurons during complex behavior?
key_assumptions: stable optical access requires reproducible skull positioning
voluntary engagement does not preclude prolonged fixation
Thy1-GCaMP6f rats provide chronic expression suitable for longitudinal imaging
key_findings: animals self-fix for 2-4 s and complete hundreds of odor-discrimination trials per session
reflectance collection scheme increases two-photon signal
same hippocampal neurons tracked for median 6 months, up to 19 months
interpretations: voluntary magnetic head-fixation enables reliable lifespan cellular imaging during behavior
approach overcomes restraint-imposed limits on behavioral repertoire and longitudinal scope
